findings.summary: RelationUtils bundles 6 unrelated concerns into a single static utility class
evidence: resolveRelationAlias / validateAliases — alias lifecycle management
findBestCollocatePattern — grammar-config search
buildFullPattern (2 overloads) — BCQL pattern construction
buildCollocateReversePattern — reverse-lookup pattern derivation
computeCollocatePosGroup + extractLabelContent + resolvePosGroupFromPrefix — POS group inference from raw pattern strings
findings.suggestion: Extract the POS-group inference helpers (computeCollocatePosGroup, extractLabelContent, resolvePosGroupFromPrefix) into a dedicated package-private class (e.g. CollocatePosResolver). Consider splitting alias management and pattern building into separate final classes so each has a single coherent responsibility.

findings.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer stores executor directly but only uses it in fetchExamples; all other methods delegate to sub-objects that each hold their own reference to the same executor
evidence: Field: private final QueryExecutor executor (line 54)
executor is passed to CollocateProfileComparator, MultiSeedExplorer, SingleSeedExplorer — each stores it internally
Only fetchExamples (line 174) calls executor.executeBcqlQuery directly on the field
exploreByRelation, exploreMultiSeed, compareCollocateProfiles never reference this.executor
findings.suggestion: Move the executeBcqlQuery call in fetchExamples to a dedicated collaborator (e.g. ExamplesQueryService) that accepts CollocateQueryPort, then remove the executor field from SemanticFieldExplorer. This eliminates the phantom shared state and makes each dependency's scope explicit.

findings.summary: executeCollocateSearchWithStoredFields and executeCollocateSearch are thin wrappers that differ only by a single boolean flag, leaking an implementation detail into the public method names
evidence: executeCollocateSearchWithStoredFields delegates to performCollocateSearch(lemma, pattern, true)
executeCollocateSearch delegates to performCollocateSearch(lemma, pattern, false)
Both public methods have no body other than the delegation
findings.suggestion: Either collapse to a single method accepting an explicit CollocateSearchMode enum, or inline the two-line wrapper bodies at call sites in BlackLabQueryExecutor to remove the indirection tier.

findings.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer (and its sub-objects) depend on QueryExecutor (full interface) when only CollocateQueryPort is needed for the one direct call in fetchExamples
evidence: SemanticFieldExplorer constructor accepts QueryExecutor executor
fetchExamples only calls executor.executeBcqlQuery() — declared on CollocateQueryPort
ConcordanceHandlers and CorpusQueryHandlers already follow the narrow-port pattern by depending on CollocateQueryPort
The exploration layer thus has a wider dependency surface than its actual usage requires
findings.suggestion: Widen the exploration layer's dependency surface only as needed: accept CollocateQueryPort for corpus-lookup operations. This mirrors the pattern ConcordanceHandlers and CorpusQueryHandlers already follow and makes the dependency boundary explicit.

findings.summary: CqlUtils.findLabelTokenIndex makes two separate passes through the pattern string: one indexOf call to find the label, then a full character scan from position 0 to count bracket tokens
evidence: int labelIndex = pattern.indexOf(labelStr) — first pass to locate label
for (int i = 0; i < pattern.length(); i++) — second pass from position 0 counting '[' characters
The token counter restarts from 0 rather than walking forward from labelIndex
findings.suggestion: Combine into a single forward pass: count '[' characters incrementally while scanning toward labelIndex; stop as soon as the matching bracket is found at or after labelIndex. This eliminates the redundant full-string re-scan.

findings.summary: executeSurfaceCollocations is declared in both SketchQueryPort and QueryExecutor (which extends it), resulting in duplicate signatures with divergent Javadoc
evidence: SketchQueryPort.executeSurfaceCollocations — concise declaration at line 30
QueryExecutor.executeSurfaceCollocations — re-declared at line 87 with a longer @implNote comment
QueryExecutor already extends SketchQueryPort, so the re-declaration is redundant
findings.suggestion: Remove the re-declaration from QueryExecutor; move any additional Javadoc into the SketchQueryPort definition. If the extended documentation is important, annotate with @see or link to SketchQueryPort from the QueryExecutor interface-level Javadoc.

findings.summary: ExplorationHandlers error cases — ExplorationException → 503, unknown relation → 400 — are not tested through the HTTP stack
evidence: ExplorationHandlers.sendExplorationError maps ExplorationException → 503 but no test injects a failing service
resolveRelationConfig throws IAE for unknown relation IDs but no test exercises this path via handleSemanticFieldExplore
handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti: no test for seeds.size() < 2 case through the HTTP handler (only service-level validation is tested)
findings.suggestion: Add tests that inject a stub ExplorationService that throws ExplorationException and verify the response is 503. Add tests for unknown relation IDs and single-seed calls to the multi endpoint and verify 400 responses.

findings.summary: SemanticFieldExplorerTest defines a local StubExecutor subclass that duplicates the shared StubQueryExecutor infrastructure
evidence: SemanticFieldExplorerTest defines `private static class StubExecutor extends StubQueryExecutor` with duplicate override pattern
StubQueryExecutor's Javadoc explicitly documents the anonymous-subclass override pattern as the intended test use
Other test files (ExplorationHandlersTest, SketchHandlersTest) use anonymous subclasses of StubQueryExecutor correctly
findings.suggestion: Remove the local StubExecutor class; replace usages with anonymous subclasses of StubQueryExecutor following the pattern already used in ExplorationHandlersTest.collocatingExecutor.
